transcript.title: Opening
transcript.text: Good morning. This is your Grace Bath World briefing for Wednesday, June 24. Over the next half hour: top headlines, bhopal and madhya pradesh, across india, around the world, business and markets, money, tax and personal finance, science and technology, development and policy. Let's begin.

transcript.title: Top headlines
transcript.text: First, the stories leading the news this morning. Delhi 10-yr-old rape and murder: How father's memory, digital trail led police to suspected cabbie. A father's fragmented memory of a white car and a yellow plate initiated a swift investigation. Investigators pieced together a digital trail, including CCTV footage and cab aggregator data, to track down Basu Kumar Singh, a driver accused of abducting, raping, and murdering a 10-year-old girl. The suspect was apprehended within seven hours of the initial report, later confessing to the heinous crime. Iran rushes to sell oil to India after Trump sanctions waiver - all you need to know. Data from Vortexa, combined with Bloomberg calculations, show that around 68 million barrels of crude and condensate were floating at sea as of June 22. More than 80 percent of that volume does not appear to have a confirmed destination, potentially making it available for sale. A tale of two deals: Trump's 'infinity' inspections meets Iran's 'not so fast'. After annus horribilis 2025, Indian DGCA to come under US FAA scanner this November. Oracle layoffs: Company confirms how many employees got 6AM email from leadership. Oracle has initiated significant layoffs globally, impacting thousands of employees across US, India, and other regions. These workforce reductions, attributed to management changes, AI adoption, and strategic shifts, resulted in a 13 percent drop in its workforce. The company incurred substantial restructuring costs, with severance packages reportedly less generous than industry peers. Bank of India wins rupees 100 crore case against Nirav Modi. FIFA WC: Ronaldo scripts history in Portugal's dominant 5-0 victory over Uzbekistan. Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice as Portugal crushed Uzbekistan 5-0 in Houston to strengthen their place in the FIFA World Cup 2026 knockout stages. The captain opened the scoring before adding a record-breaking second goal, becoming the first player to score in six consecutive World Cups and surpassing Eusébio as Portugal's all-time leading World Cup scorer. Flu kills 1.2 lakh/year in India, senior citizens bear the brunt. After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires. A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion—and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15 percent of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker. 'She pushed him, then blamed a snake': Pune man's father claims fiancée's first murder bid failed. A Pune businessman, initially thought to have died in a trekking accident, was allegedly murdered by his fiancée and her lover. Police arrested Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary for pushing Ketan Agarwal into a gorge at Lohagad Fort. The couple was set to marry in November. Stampede-like situation at Ranchi stadium during Jharkhand T20 League final; several injured. Overseas branches can lend to NRIs for deposits in India. RBI Clarifies FCNR(B) Rules, NRI Inflows To Support Rupee After laying off hundreds of engineers, GM now replaces more than 1,000 workers with 50 robots. General Motors has introduced 50 collaborative robots at its Michigan Factory Zero, reportedly leading to over 1,000 job eliminations. This move, aimed at cost-cutting and efficiency, sparks criticism from the United Auto Workers union, which views it as a threat to jobs. GM claims the robots support human workers, but the UAW president calls it a "fight for humanity" amid broader automation concerns in the industry. GE sends AI 171 engine analysis, final AAIB report likely in 2 months. GE Aerospace has sent the analysis reports for the engines of AI171 that had crashed in Ahmedabad last June, killing 260 people. All eyes are now on the Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau. "Air accident investigations have to zero in on what could have happened. Guwahati dropped from Japan PM's itinerary; summit shifted to Delhi. How proposed US citizenship fee hike will impact applicants; Indians among hardest hit. US Citizenship and Immigration Services plans a significant hike in naturalisation application fees, potentially costing Indian immigrants much more to become American citizens. The proposed increase for the primary application form could reach $1,330, with reduced-fee options and waivers being eliminated. This comes as Indians remain a major group seeking US citizenship. Q-commerce growth: Flipkart, Amazon bet on large base. Taylor Swift is reportedly struggling with the Blake Lively fallout. Taylor Swift and Blake Lively's bond has only worsened over the last few months as rumors swirl about the actress being invited to the pop star's wedding to Travis Kelce which is set to happen in the next few weeks. While Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce focus on their wedding, it seems the pop star has gone through a wave of emotions. The Vikings and Lions are linked to a potential trade for Colts standout. The Indianapolis Colts' star player Anthony Richardson has been actively seeking a trade from the team since February 2026 which has raised eyebrows and sparked chaos about his future in the league. Amid this, the Detroit Lions and the Minnesota Vikings have emerged as potential landing spots for him ahead of the new season. When breath turned pungent mid-sleep: Ammonia leak survivors recount ordeal. A devastating ammonia leak at a seafood processing factory in Tiruvallur district left workers, many young women far from home, in critical condition. The gas, originating from the processing area, seeped into sleeping quarters, incapacitating those off-duty. Emergency services faced delays, with many suffering severe respiratory distress and other ailments. Tom Brady sends clear message after Gisele's alleged Father's Day snub. Tom Brady, the seven time Super Bowl champion, has had a pretty successful career but his past marriage with ex wife, Gisele Bundchen, has continued to dominate headlines even after years of their divorce. The Brazilian supermodel Gisele Bundchen recently made a controversial post for Father's Day and it seems the NFL legend Tom Brady has made it clear what his current priorities are. Infosys sees $300-400billion AI opportunity by 2030. Hundreds of schools plan closures ahead of red heat alerts. The temperature topped 34.6 C in Wisley, England, while Scotland and Northern Ireland saw their hottest days of the year. Illegal immigrants from Banglaesh, Myanmar | Pushback, detention and deportation laws explained. What is a pushback? Is it same as deportation? Is it legal?

transcript.title: Across India
transcript.text: Turning to the big national stories. Cabbie abducts, rapes and kills 10-year-old in Delhi; shot by cops during escape bid. Ladakh bandh to protest 'breach' of agreement in Delhi talks. Poland donor's stem cells save Kashmir boy suffering from rare immune condition. Kolkata Port, Adani Ports sign MoU to restore iconic Kumartuli Ghat ahead of Durga Puja. rupees 89,000-crore import bill sparks fresh push for made-in-India medical devices. 'Students' blood on his hands': CJP chief Dipke hits back at Pradhan during Jantar Mantar protest. NHAI signs MoU with NCAER for research centre on economics of transportation, mobility and logistics. Qatar Emir dials PM Modi after Ras Laffan blast kills 12 Indians. 'Will face same fate as TMC and Shiv Sena': BJP claims several BJD leaders 'keen' to jump ship in Odisha. Voter ID row: Third non-bailable warrant issued against actor Prakash Raj. Cancelled Bali trip and a missing passport: Inside a fiancée and her lovers' plan to kill Pune man. 315 districts mapped for contingency measures to rescue farming operation as rain deficit rises to 43 percent. 111 districts identified as the most vulnerable due to low irrigation footprints. Delhi CM orders graft probe against two senior health officials over medicine procurement. Shah directs Nafed, NCCF to eliminate middlemen, buy pulses and oilseeds directly from farmers. Union Minister Amit Shah has directed Nafed and NCCF to procure all pulses and oilseeds directly from farmers, eliminating middlemen and ensuring payments within 48 hours. This initiative aims to guarantee fair prices, boost pulse cultivation, and achieve self-reliance. Shah also announced a two-year deadline for this direct farmer-to-organization sales model and highlighted Nafed's new digital auction platform, scholarship program, and inventory management tools. Graduates 44 percent less likely to be hired in informal sector: Report. Dhurandhars or traitors: How Eknath Shinde's 'Operation Tiger' has changed Maharashtra's power dynamics. Maharashtra's political scene is rapidly changing, as the recent defection of six Uddhav Sena MPs to Eknath Shinde's faction under 'Operation Tiger' highlights. This trend reflects a broader pattern seen in Trinamool Congress and AAP, where the anti-defection law is being sidestepped, and parties blatantly promote shifts in loyalty. The fear of the Samajwadi Party and the NCP being the next targets in this game of defections shows the vulnerability of regional parties in this political maneuvering. Gold loan default ratio shrinks as sanctions double. PM Modi calls on President Droupadi Murmu amid Cabinet reshuffle buzz. Speculation is rife about a Union Cabinet reshuffle following Prime Minister Narendra Modi's meeting with President Droupadi Murmu. Recent appointments of Union ministers to key BJP organizational roles, coupled with the resignation of George Kurian and the non-renomination of Ravneet Singh to the Rajya Sabha, have intensified these discussions. These developments suggest potential changes within the government's ministerial ranks. 5 Arrested For Chicken-And-Beer Party On Boat In Varanasi's Ganga. The police said that individuals involved were identified, sent to jail, and the boat was seized. Cliffs, 'Falls', And Lovers: Fort Murder's Parallels With Meghalaya Killing. For now, Sonam sits in jail awaiting her fate. What awaits Siya Goyal remains to be determined. US Envoy Underscores Positive Ties With India, Highlights Tamil Nadu's Role. Sergio Gor highlighted Tamil Nadu's growing importance in India Ajit Doval Says Stable India-China Relations Key To Deeper Trust. India hosted the two-day BRICS National Security Advisors' Meeting in New Delhi Kingpin In Fake Arms Licence Racket, 8 Others Arrested In Uttarakhand. Investigators said Sharma had provided many people with fake licences. Weather Highlights: Delhi Records Maximum Temperature Of 38.4 Degrees, Air Quality Deteriorates To 'Poor'. Weather Highlights: The forecast for today shows partly cloudy skies are expected in parts of Delhi. 3 Injured In Stampede Outside Stadium In Ranchi. The match was not interrupted due to the incident that occurred outside the stadium.

transcript.title: Around the world
transcript.text: And here is what is happening around the world. Israeli troops kill two in south Lebanon after lull in fighting, authorities say. Israel's military says the two men were Hezbollah operatives, but the Iran-backed group accuses it of a ceasefire violation. Dozens of ships head through Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran deal. Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone. UN commission of inquiry says Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children. Israel rejects the new report by the three-member expert panel, calling it a "libellous sham". Zambia ex-president's family wins latest legal battle over what should happen to his body. Edgar Lungu's family and the Zambian government remained in dispute 12 months after he died. Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba sues US government over defence blacklist. It is suing the US defence department after it was added to a list of firms with ties to the Chinese military. Spider which uses spring trap to capture prey discovered in Australia. Researchers say their hunting method - which allows them to prey on dangerous ants - is unprecedented. US top court says Rastafarian man cannot sue prison guards who cut his dreadlocks. The former Louisiana inmate argued his Rastafarian faith was violated after prison officials focibly shaved off his hair. Irish government to contribute £197m to cross-border rail services. The money will come from the last allocation from the Irish government's Shared Island Fund. Trump anticipates better relationship with Colombia under new leader. Abelardo de la Espriella, who preliminary results suggest is Colombia's next president, had Trump's endorsement. Montreal shooting leaves officer, civilian and suspect dead. It was the first time in 24 years that a Montreal police officer was killed in the line of duty, the city's police chief said. Ransom note claims Nancy Guthrie died after abduction. On Tuesday, Today show presenter Savannah Guthrie begged anyone with information to come forward, saying, "We are in agony." Iran says no new commitments on nuclear sites after Vance says inspectors to be invited back. Iran's foreign ministry says it made "no new commitments" on nuclear inspections after talks in Switzerland. Dettol apologises after ad to clean up 'toxic men' backfires in China. The ad sparked accusations of sexism in China, after featuring a man looking for a partner who is "not tainted by other men". Inquiry ordered after building fire kills 15 in north India city. Witnesses described people jumping from windows to escape, while rescue teams broke through a wall to save lives. Myanmar army killed over 700 civilians in six months, UN says. The new UN report says the 702 civilian deaths over six months last year included 153 children. Russian troop build-up threatens city seen as key to seizing Ukraine's Donbas. If Kostyantynivka falls, Russian forces would be able push towards Ukraine's last remaining strongholds in the east. Tesla crash that killed a woman under US federal investigation. A Tesla driver on Friday said he was using 'self-driving' technology after his vehicle sped into a Texas home. At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion. The city's main liquified natural gas (LNG) processing site suffered a "technical accident" in the Ras Laffan industrial zone. Kenya minister halts construction of US-backed Ebola quarantine centre. Aden Duale defied a High Court order to stop building work on a controversial US-funded facility. UN begins evacuation of 11,000 sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz. The UN's maritime agency launched an operation to evacuate more than 11,000 sailors stranded in the Strait of Hormuz. Kenyan minister orders halt to construction of US Ebola facility. Decision comes after Aden Duale was held in contempt for ignoring previous high court ruling to stop work Kenya's health minister told a court he had ordered preparations for a US-run Ebola quarantine facility to stop, after being held in contempt for ignoring a previous order to end work. Many Kenyans strongly oppose the facility , with deadly protests erupting since the complex was announced in May for US citizens evacuated from the Democratic Republic of Congo, which is grappling with a widespread Ebola outbreak . Continue reading... Ronaldo late to World Cup party but still able to steal the show. Portugal coach Martinez says Ronaldo's rivalry with the likes of Messi drives both players to stay at their best. UK prioritised ties with UAE over averting mass atrocities in Sudan, MPs to be told. Foreign Office failed to act on warnings of genocide due to 'pressure' from emirates, Yale human rights investigator will tell a parliamentary select committee The British government had received intelligence that Ethiopia appeared to be supporting a genocidal militia in Sudan's civil war as far back as 2024 but did not go public with the news for fear of upsetting the United Arab Emirates (UAE), a parliamentary committee will hear. In May 2024, officials from the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) told Nathaniel Raymond , an American human rights investigator at Yale University, that "significant private pressure" from the UAE meant the UK would not publicly divulge information linking Ethiopia and the emirates to their support for the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces. Continue reading... Protesters sentenced to decades in US prison over alleged antifa ties. Civil liberties advocates have expressed alarm over the case involving a protest outside Prairieland Detention Center.
